{{Quote|I am sending you this small toy, so that you will remember me and what I stood for.|[[Morvet Storm]]|<ref name="The Lost Jedi Adventure Game Book">''[[The Lost Jedi Adventure Game Book]]''</ref>}}
'''Arf''' was the nickname given to a [[K-9 series hunting-and-tracking droid|K-9]] series [[Droid/Legends|droid]] owned by [[Havet Storm]]. Little more than a toy Arf was given to Havet by his grandfather [[Morvet Storm]]. A [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Morvet used the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to record a secret audio message for his grandson, telling him of his potential to become a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and warning him of the dangers of revealing his abilities to others.

[[Morvet Storm]] also hid within the K-9 a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], which remained hidden inside Arf until [[Havet Storm]] received the droid and activated Morvet's secure message by touching a DNA sample reader located on the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] casing.

Arf proved to be invaluable to [[Havet Storm]] on a number of occasions, who kept the droid with him at all times, hidden within a large pocket in his bulky coat. The droid was extremely loyal, responding only to his master's voice and the two formed a close bond.
